title: IELTS Academic Reading April 2026 Passage 2 - The Psychology of Cancel Culture
rawPassageText:
READING PASSAGE 2
You should spend about 20 minutes on Questions 14-26, which are based on Reading Passage 2 below.

The Psychology of Cancel Culture

A

Cancel culture is a modern label for a much older social habit: communities have always rewarded some behaviour and punished other behaviour through approval, gossip, exclusion or shame. What has changed is the speed and scale of public judgement. A remark once heard by a few people can now be copied, reposted and discussed by thousands within hours. Online platforms also make social punishment visible. People can see not only the original offence, but also the number of users condemning it. This public visibility can make a dispute feel larger and more urgent than it first was.

B

Psychologists often connect cancel culture with moral emotion. Anger and disgust can help people notice harm and defend shared values. Public outrage may also signal loyalty to a group: by condemning a statement, a user shows others which side they are on. This signalling is not always cynical. Many people genuinely believe they are protecting vulnerable groups or enforcing standards of decency. However, moral emotion can narrow attention. Once a person is seen as a symbol of wrongdoing, it becomes harder for observers to notice context, uncertainty or later change.

C

The consequences of cancellation are not always the same. In some cases, public criticism exposes behaviour that institutions ignored and gives victims a way to be heard. In other cases, punishment can be disproportionate, especially when a careless phrase is treated like a serious pattern of abuse. The status of the person accused, the seriousness of the harm and the possibility of repair all matter. For this reason, it is misleading to describe cancel culture as either pure accountability or pure cruelty. It can contain elements of both.

D

Social media also creates what researchers call context collapse. A comment intended for one audience can suddenly be judged by a very different audience. An old joke, a classroom remark or a private message may be separated from its original setting and circulated as a screenshot. Viewers who were not present may fill in missing details with their own assumptions. This does not mean that harmful comments should be excused. It means that digital records often travel without the tone, relationship or situation that originally shaped their meaning.

E

Cancel culture affects bystanders as well as the person being criticised. Some users join the criticism because they agree with it; others join because silence may look like approval of the offence. A third group says nothing at all because they fear becoming targets themselves. This fear can produce what psychologists call preference falsification, when people hide their private doubts and publicly support the opinion that seems safest. As a result, online agreement may appear stronger than it really is, while more complicated views remain unspoken.

F

Apologies play a complicated role. A quick apology can sound empty if it only tries to end the criticism. A defensive apology may make the situation worse by shifting blame to the audience or claiming that everyone misunderstood. Research on conflict repair suggests that apologies work better when they name the harm, accept responsibility and offer a concrete form of repair. Even then, forgiveness cannot be demanded. An apology is a step in a social process, not a button that automatically restores trust.

G

Some writers argue for slower and more restorative responses. Before demanding punishment, communities can verify claims, separate intent from impact and ask what repair would look like. Schools, workplaces and professional organisations may need processes that protect complainants while also allowing accused people to respond. These slower procedures can feel unsatisfying in fast-moving online spaces, but they may reduce mistakes and make learning possible. The aim is not to remove consequences. It is to make consequences more accurate, proportionate and useful.

Questions 14-20

Reading Passage 2 has seven paragraphs, A-G.

Which paragraph contains the following information?

Write the correct letter, A-G, in boxes 14-20 on your answer sheet.

14 reference to social punishment as an old practice made faster and larger online
15 explanation that public anger can show loyalty to a social group
16 acknowledgement that cancellation can reveal harm but may also become excessive
17 reference to an old comment being judged by people outside its original setting
18 mention of people avoiding speech because they fear becoming targets
19 description of apology features that are more likely to repair conflict
20 suggestion that slower procedures may lead to more accurate consequences

Questions 21-24

Complete the summary below.

Choose ONE WORD ONLY from the passage for each answer.

Write your answers in boxes 21-24 on your answer sheet.

Social judgement online

Cancel culture usually involves public judgement and visible social 21 .................... . Moral outrage may signal 22 .................... to a group. However, online records can travel without the original 23 .................... that shaped their meaning. Some bystanders hide their private doubts and publicly support the opinion that seems 24 .................... .

Questions 25 and 26

Choose TWO letters, A-H.

Which TWO statements are true according to the passage?

A Cancel culture is described as a completely new form of social behaviour.
B Public criticism can sometimes expose behaviour that institutions ignored.
C Context is always preserved when comments are shared online.
D Defensive apologies may make criticism worse.
E The writer says cancellation is always pure cruelty.
F Preference falsification means people always state their private views honestly.
G The passage argues that consequences should be more accurate and proportionate.
H Forgiveness must always follow immediately after an apology.
rawAnswerKey:
Question 14: reference to social punishment as an old practice made faster and larger online

Correct Answer: A

Accepted Answers: A

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading April 2026 Passage 2 - The Psychology of Cancel Culture

Exact Portion: Cancel culture is a modern label for a much older social habit: communities have always rewarded some behaviour and punished other behaviour through approval, gossip, exclusion or shame. What has changed is the speed and scale of public judgement.

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ A เพราะย่อหน้า A บอกว่า cancel culture เป็น modern label สำหรับ much older social habit และพูดถึงการ punish behaviour ผ่าน gossip, exclusion หรือ shame จากนั้นบอกว่า what has changed คือ speed and scale of public judgement โจทย์ถาม social punishment ที่เป็น old practice แต่ถูกทำให้เร็วและใหญ่ขึ้น online ซึ่งสรุปใจความของย่อหน้า A ได้ครบที่สุด

Paraphrased Vocabulary: social punishment = punished behaviour through approval, gossip, exclusion or shame; old practice = much older social habit; faster and larger = speed and scale; answer signal = A

Question 15: explanation that public anger can show loyalty to a social group

Correct Answer: B

Accepted Answers: B

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading April 2026 Passage 2 - The Psychology of Cancel Culture

Exact Portion: Public outrage may also signal loyalty to a group: by condemning a statement, a user shows others which side they are on.

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ B เพราะย่อหน้า B อธิบาย moral emotion และบอกตรง ๆ ว่า public outrage may signal loyalty to a group โดยการประณาม statement ผู้ใช้แสดงให้คนอื่นเห็นว่าอยู่ฝ่ายไหน โจทย์ใช้คำว่า public anger ซึ่ง paraphrase จาก public outrage และใช้คำว่า social group ซึ่งตรงกับ group ใน passage จึงตอบ B

Paraphrased Vocabulary: public anger = public outrage; show loyalty = signal loyalty; social group = group / which side they are on; answer signal = B

Question 16: acknowledgement that cancellation can reveal harm but may also become excessive

Correct Answer: C

Accepted Answers: C

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading April 2026 Passage 2 - The Psychology of Cancel Culture

Exact Portion: In some cases, public criticism exposes behaviour that institutions ignored and gives victims a way to be heard. In other cases, punishment can be disproportionate

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ C เพราะย่อหน้า C ให้ภาพสองด้านของ cancellation โดยบอกว่าในบางกรณี public criticism exposes behaviour that institutions ignored แต่ในบางกรณี punishment can be disproportionate โจทย์ถามว่า cancellation can reveal harm แต่ may also become excessive ซึ่งตรงกับ exposes behaviour และ disproportionate punishment ในย่อหน้า C

Paraphrased Vocabulary: reveal harm = exposes behaviour that institutions ignored / gives victims a way to be heard; excessive = disproportionate; cancellation = public criticism / punishment; answer signal = C

Question 17: reference to an old comment being judged by people outside its original setting

Correct Answer: D

Accepted Answers: D

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading April 2026 Passage 2 - The Psychology of Cancel Culture

Exact Portion: An old joke, a classroom remark or a private message may be separated from its original setting and circulated as a screenshot.

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ D เพราะย่อหน้า D พูดเรื่อง context collapse โดยยกตัวอย่างว่า old joke, classroom remark หรือ private message อาจถูก separated from its original setting แล้วถูกส่งต่อเป็น screenshot โจทย์ถามเรื่อง old comment ที่ถูกตัดสินโดยคนที่อยู่นอกบริบทเดิม ซึ่งตรงกับแนวคิดว่า viewers who were not present อาจเติมรายละเอียดเองจาก assumptions ดังนั้นข้อมูลนี้อยู่ในย่อหน้า D ชัดเจนที่สุด

Paraphrased Vocabulary: old comment = old joke / classroom remark / private message; outside original setting = separated from its original setting / viewers who were not present; answer signal = D

Question 18: mention of people avoiding speech because they fear becoming targets

Correct Answer: E

Accepted Answers: E

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading April 2026 Passage 2 - The Psychology of Cancel Culture

Exact Portion: A third group says nothing at all because they fear becoming targets themselves.

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ E เพราะย่อหน้า E กล่าวถึง bystanders หลายกลุ่ม และระบุชัดว่า a third group says nothing at all because they fear becoming targets themselves โจทย์ถามว่าคนหลีกเลี่ยงการพูดเพราะกลัวกลายเป็นเป้าหมาย ซึ่งตรงกับ says nothing และ fear becoming targets ในย่อหน้า E โดยตรง

Paraphrased Vocabulary: avoiding speech = says nothing at all; fear becoming targets = fear becoming targets themselves; people = a third group / bystanders; answer signal = E

Question 19: description of apology features that are more likely to repair conflict

Correct Answer: F

Accepted Answers: F

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading April 2026 Passage 2 - The Psychology of Cancel Culture

Exact Portion: apologies work better when they name the harm, accept responsibility and offer a concrete form of repair.

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ F เพราะย่อหน้า F พูดเรื่อง apologies และระบุคุณลักษณะที่ทำให้ apology work better ได้แก่ name the harm, accept responsibility และ offer a concrete form of repair โจทย์ถาม apology features ที่มีแนวโน้มจะ repair conflict ซึ่งตรงกับ conflict repair และ work better ในย่อหน้า F จึงตอบ F

Paraphrased Vocabulary: apology features = name the harm / accept responsibility / offer repair; more likely to repair conflict = work better / conflict repair; answer signal = F

Question 20: suggestion that slower procedures may lead to more accurate consequences

Correct Answer: G

Accepted Answers: G

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading April 2026 Passage 2 - The Psychology of Cancel Culture

Exact Portion: These slower procedures can feel unsatisfying in fast-moving online spaces, but they may reduce mistakes and make learning possible. The aim is not to remove consequences. It is to make consequences more accurate, proportionate and useful.

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ G เพราะย่อหน้า G เสนอ slower and more restorative responses และบอกว่า slower procedures อาจ reduce mistakes และทำให้ consequences more accurate, proportionate and useful โจทย์ถาม slower procedures ที่นำไปสู่ more accurate consequences ซึ่งตรงกับย่อหน้า G โดยตรง

Paraphrased Vocabulary: slower procedures = slower and more restorative responses / slower procedures; more accurate consequences = consequences more accurate, proportionate and useful; answer signal = G

Question 21: Cancel culture usually involves public judgement and visible social .................... .

Correct Answer: punishment

Accepted Answers: punishment

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading April 2026 Passage 2 - The Psychology of Cancel Culture

Exact Portion: Online platforms also make social punishment visible.

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ punishment เพราะ summary ถามว่า cancel culture involves public judgement and visible social ____ ส่วน passage ระบุว่า online platforms also make social punishment visible คำว่า visible social ใน summary เหมือนกับ social punishment visible ใน passage เพียงเปลี่ยนลำดับคำ คำที่ต้องเติมเป็นคำเดียวจาก passage คือ punishment

Paraphrased Vocabulary: visible social punishment = make social punishment visible; public judgement = public judgement; answer signal = punishment

Question 22: Moral outrage may signal .................... to a group.

Correct Answer: loyalty

Accepted Answers: loyalty

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading April 2026 Passage 2 - The Psychology of Cancel Culture

Exact Portion: Public outrage may also signal loyalty to a group

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ loyalty เพราะ summary บอกว่า Moral outrage may signal ____ to a group และ passage ใช้วลีตรงมากว่า Public outrage may also signal loyalty to a group คำว่า moral outrage ใน summary เทียบกับ public outrage ใน passage ได้ และคำตอบคือ loyalty ซึ่งเป็นคำเดียวจาก passage

Paraphrased Vocabulary: moral outrage = public outrage; may signal = may signal; to a group = to a group; answer signal = loyalty

Question 23: However, online records can travel without the original .................... that shaped their meaning.

Correct Answer: situation

Accepted Answers: situation

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading April 2026 Passage 2 - The Psychology of Cancel Culture

Exact Portion: digital records often travel without the tone, relationship or situation that originally shaped their meaning.

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ situation เพราะ summary ถามว่า online records can travel without the original ____ that shaped their meaning และ passage ระบุว่า digital records often travel without the tone, relationship or situation that originally shaped their meaning ใน summary ใช้ the original เพื่อรวมความหมายว่า originally shaped ดังนั้นคำที่เติมได้ตรงและเป็นคำเดียวคือ situation

Paraphrased Vocabulary: online records = digital records; travel without = travel without; shaped their meaning = originally shaped their meaning; answer signal = situation

Question 24: Some bystanders hide their private doubts and publicly support the opinion that seems .................... .

Correct Answer: safest

Accepted Answers: safest

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading April 2026 Passage 2 - The Psychology of Cancel Culture

Exact Portion: people hide their private doubts and publicly support the opinion that seems safest.

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ safest เพราะ summary บอกว่า bystanders hide their private doubts และ publicly support the opinion that seems ____ ส่วน passage ระบุประโยคตรงกันว่า people hide their private doubts and publicly support the opinion that seems safest ความหมายคือคนเลือกแสดงความเห็นที่ดูปลอดภัยที่สุดในพื้นที่สาธารณะ คำตอบที่เป็นคำเดียวจาก passage คือ safest

Paraphrased Vocabulary: bystanders = people / a third group; hide private doubts = hide their private doubts; publicly support = publicly support; answer signal = safest

Question 25: Which TWO statements are true according to the passage?

Correct Answer: B

Accepted Answers: B

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading April 2026 Passage 2 - The Psychology of Cancel Culture

Exact Portion: public criticism exposes behaviour that institutions ignored and gives victims a way to be heard.

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบหนึ่งคือ B เพราะ passage กล่าวตรง ๆ ว่า public criticism exposes behaviour that institutions ignored and gives victims a way to be heard ซึ่งตรงกับตัวเลือก B ที่บอกว่า public criticism can sometimes expose behaviour that institutions ignored คำว่า sometimes ในตัวเลือกตรงกับ In some cases ใน passage จึงเป็น statement ที่ true ตามบทความ

Paraphrased Vocabulary: public criticism = public criticism; can sometimes = in some cases; exposes behaviour institutions ignored = exposes behaviour that institutions ignored; answer signal = B

Question 26: Which TWO statements are true according to the passage?

Correct Answer: G

Accepted Answers: G

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading April 2026 Passage 2 - The Psychology of Cancel Culture

Exact Portion: It is to make consequences more accurate, proportionate and useful.

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบอีกข้อคือ G เพราะ passage สรุปเป้าหมายของ slower/restorative responses ว่าไม่ใช่การ remove consequences แต่ทำให้ consequences more accurate, proportionate and useful ตัวเลือก G บอกว่า writer argues consequences should be more accurate and proportionate ซึ่งตรงกับ passage โดยตรง จึงเป็นอีก statement ที่ true ตามบทความ

Paraphrased Vocabulary: consequences should be = the aim is to make consequences; more accurate and proportionate = more accurate, proportionate and useful; answer signal = G
